code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test25-NOV-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal pathology or dehydration. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating no hyperglycemia. Recommend further evaluation for renal impairment and possible causes of proteinuria and ketonuria.
